data.title: Trump Threatens Tariffs on Canadian Lumber and Dairy Imports as Early as Today
data.description:
President Donald Trump announced that he is considering imposing reciprocal tariffs on Canadian lumber and dairy imports as early as today, although he also mentioned that they might be delayed until Monday or Tuesday. He argued that these measures would mirror Canada’s own trade restrictions and tariffs on U.S. goods. Speaking from the Oval Office, Trump stated, âTheyâll be met with the exact same tariff unless they drop it.â

This announcement comes after Trump decided to postpone previously planned tariffs on Canadian and Mexican imports tied to the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA). The USMCA, which is a key trade deal that was negotiated during his first term, was designed to update the trade rules between the three North American countries and replace the North American Free Trade Agreement. Nevertheless, Trump continues to criticize Canadaâs trade policies.

This is especially true when it comes to lumber and dairy because he believes that they put U.S. producers at a disadvantage. âThey make it impossible for us to sell lumber or dairy products into Canada,â he said. His comments continue to add to the trade tensions between the two countries and continue to make businesses that rely on cross-border trade more nervous.

Is WY a Good Investment?

As a lumber producer with operations in both Canada and the U.S., Weyerhaeuser (WY) is a stock that would be impacted by Trump’s latest tariff threats. Nevertheless, Wall Street analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on WY stock based on seven Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10% decline in its share price over the past year, the average Weyerhaeuser price target of $35 per share implies 14.3% upside potential.

See more WY analyst ratings
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Ethereum’s ‘Pectra Upgrade’ Postponed After Failed Tests
data.description:
A highly anticipated upgrade to the Ethereum (ETH) blockchain network has been postponed following disappointing initial tests.

The Pectra upgrade, which is Ethereum’s biggest upgrade in a year, has been put off following whatâs being described as two âbuggy test runs.â Consequently, network developers have decided to postpone the upgrade pending further trials.

The Pectra upgrade is designed to improve Ethereum’s speed and ease of use. The decision to postpone the upgrade comes after Pectra’s tests on Holesky and Sepolia, Ethereum’s two primary test networks, each encountered bugs.

Additional Tests Needed

Developers say that Pectra needs to undergo additional testing before it goes live on Ethereum’s main network. It is not clear when the Pectra upgrade will now be completed. However, Pectra is set to be one of the Ethereum blockchainâs most ambitious and consequential changes to date.

The upgrade is aimed at improving ease of use for both users and operators of the network. The upgrade is also supposed to give crypto wallets some smart contract capabilities. Ethereum is trying to better compete against other cryptocurrencies such as Solana (SOL).

The price of Ethereum has declined 34% so far this year.

Is ETH a Buy?

Most Wall Street firms donât offer ratings or price targets on Ethereum , so weâll look at the cryptocurrencyâs three-month performance instead. As one can see in the chart below, the price of ETH has declined 45% in the last 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: “I’m in This for the Long Haul,” Says Billionaire Investor amid Market Volatility
data.description:
Despite Trump’s chaotic tariff policies, long-term investors remain calm and collected. Indeed, according to Billionaire investor David Booth, who is the founder and chair of Dimensional Fund Advisors, his clients are focused on the big picture and aren’t panicking amid the market’s volatility. “What we have are clients that are really long-term investors… I’m in this for the long haul,” Booth told Yahoo Finance.

Booth’s approach to investing is based on his experience and education. His new documentary, Tune Out the Noise, takes a look at his career and the creation of Dimensional Fund Advisors, which now manages over $777 billion in assets. Booth’s investment strategy is built on discipline and a long-term perspective. This has helped his firm become a global leader in investment management.

Booth’s advice to investors is to tune out the noise and focus on the fundamentals. Indeed, he believes that markets are driven by buyers and sellers making informed decisions rather than by political parties or congressional leaders. By taking a step back and looking at the bigger picture, investors can make more informed decisions and avoid getting caught up in short-term market fluctuations.

Is SPY a Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY)Â based on 413 Buys, 86 Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After aÂ 13% rally in its share priceÂ over the past year, theÂ average SPY price targetÂ of $691.20 per share implies 21.3% upside potential.

See SPYâs holdings
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Intuitive Machines (LUNR) Stock Crashes 25% on News of Moon Mishap
data.description:
The stock of Intuitive Machines (LUNR) is down 25% on reports that the space exploration company experienced difficulties with a recent moon landing.

Specifically, there are media reports that Intuitive Machines’ Athena spacecraft had trouble landing near the moonâs south pole. The spaceship touched down on the lunar surface, but NASAâs broadcast of the landing ended with no clear information about the current status of the craft and whether it is upright and its systems operational.

Intuitive CEO Steve Altemus said at a press conference that he doesnât believe the Athena spacecraft is upright. “I think we can all agree â¦ that landing on the moon is extremely hard, and Intuitive Machines was aiming to land in a place that humanity has not been before,” said Nicola Fox, NASAâs associate director at the Science Missions Directorate at the joint press conference.

Bad for Shareholders

In a positive development, NASA says that the Athena spacecraft is returning some data back from the moon’s surface following the landing. Athena is the companyâs second landing on the moon’s surface. The previous Odysseus lander touched down on the moon in February 2024.

NASA called last year’s Odysseus mission a “success.” It was the first time the U.S. had landed on the moon in 50-plus years. However, the Odysseus spacecraft also tipped over upon landing on the moon. Both landings were part of NASAâs Commercial Lunar Payload Services program, which aims to have NASA contract for payload deliveries to the lunar surface.

Intuitive Machines is seen as being at the forefront of the commercial space industry. LUNR stock has risen 62% over the last 12 months.

Is LUNR Stock a Buy?

The stock of Intuitive Machines has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among six Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy, one Hold, and one sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LUNR price target of $17.67 implies 100.80% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on LUNR stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: XRP’s Major Upgrade Brings NFTs and Security—Will Price React?
data.description:
The XRP (XRP-USD) Ledger is leveling up. RippleX has released rippled version 2.4.0. This brings key changes aimed at boosting security, compliance, and overall network efficiency. According to RippleX, the upgrade includes new features like DynamicNFT (XLS-46). This allows for mutable NFTs that can have their metadata updated after issuance. But will these changes impact XRPâs price? Before anything goes live, the amendments still need 80% approval from validators over two consecutive weeks.

New Features Strengthen Compliance and Security

This update isnât just about NFTs. The PermissionedDomains (XLS-80) amendment enhances compliance by restricting domain access within the XRP ecosystem. Another major change, DeepFreeze (XLS-77), allows issuers to freeze certain token transfers, a move that aligns with regulatory expectations. Meanwhile, fixFrozenLPTokenTransfer prevents liquidity pool (LP) tokens from being moved if one of the assets in the pool is frozen.

Beyond security and compliance, RippleX has fine-tuned the networkâs performance. A new simulated API method lets developers test transactions before they go live, reducing costly errors. The update also fixes long-standing bugs, including issues with Windows and MacOS builds, as well as problems with inbound ledger acquisitions and transaction fees.

Could This Impact XRPâs Price?

XRP traders are watching to see how these changes will influence price action. Historically, major upgrades to the XRP Ledger have fueled speculation and increased trading volume. However, the bigger factor could be todayâs White House Crypto Summit. President Trump just signed an executive order establishing the U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, but XRP didnât make the cut. Instead, it was placed in the broader U.S. Digital Asset Stockpile, which lacks the same level of commitment.

While this signals some level of government acknowledgment, it doesnât guarantee institutional buying pressure like Bitcoin might see. For now, XRPâs price direction could hinge on market reaction to both this upgrade and any policy signals coming out of Washington.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $2.5030.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Wayfair (W) Stock Can Rally 40% Higher, Says Jefferies
data.description:
The stock of furniture retailer Wayfair (W) can rally 40% higher in coming months, according to a bullish note from U.S. investment bank Jefferies Financial Group (JEF).

Jonathan Matuszewski, a top four-star rated analyst, says that Wayfair, which specializes in selling furniture and home goods online, has several “green shoots” that should power the stock higher this year. He has upgraded W stock to a Buy rating from Hold previously and raised his price target on the shares to $47 from $45. The new price target is about 40% above where the stock finished trading on March 6.

“With top-tier market share gains, a new paid loyalty program, compelling B2B traction, and underrated physical retail expansion, we have fresh optimism for EBITDA growth above the Street,” wrote Matuszewski in a note to clients.

Attractive Valuation

Matuszewski added that W stock is attractively valued right now, trading near its 52-week low and at about the same level as when the Covid-19 pandemic was first declared in 2020. Wayfair has a market capitalization of only $4.38 billion, making it a small-cap stock defined as any security with a market value below $10 billion.

In his note, Matuszewski added that year-over-year increases in existing home sales indicate housing momentum in the U.S., which should provide a tailwind for sales of Wayfair’s furniture and home goods. Wayfairâs newly-launched paid loyalty program is another positive for the company and could help drive the share price higher in coming months.

News of the analyst upgrade comes on the same day that it was disclosed Wayfair plans to cut 340 staff from its technology team. W stock has declined 23% so far this year.

Is W Stock a Buy?

The stock of Wayfair has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 23 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy and 10 Hold recommendations assigned in the past three months. The average W price target of $53.14 implies 55.24% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on W stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Bitcoin Is Back in the Game as U.S. Dollar Takes a Historic Fall
data.description:
Bitcoin is bouncing back after a sharp decline, and a weakening U.S. dollar may be setting the stage for a bigger move. The U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) just posted one of its biggest weekly drops in over a decade, and history suggests that this kind of plunge often signals a Bitcoin bottom. According to Global Macro Investor, the DXYâs one-week percentage drop exceeded a negative four standard deviation moveâan event that has only happened three other times since 2013. Each time, Bitcoin rebounded significantly.

DXY Decline Often Marks Bitcoin Bottoms

Previous instances of such sharp dollar declines occurred in 2015, 2020, and 2022âeach aligning with a major Bitcoin price recovery. In November 2022, Bitcoin hit a cycle low of $15,500 during the FTX collapse. In March 2020, it briefly fell below $5,000 amid COVID-19 market chaos. And in the 2015 bear market, Bitcoin was trading around $250 before rallying. A weaker dollar often benefits risk assets, and with the DXY currently at 103.8, some analysts see an opportunity for Bitcoinâs next leg higher.

Bitcoin Bounces 4% after Strategic Reserve âSell the Newsâ Drop

Bitcoin initially dropped following President Donald Trumpâs announcement of the U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, a move that disappointed traders hoping for fresh government Bitcoin purchases. Instead, the reserve will only hold confiscated Bitcoin, which triggered a âsell the newsâ reaction. However, Bitcoin has since rebounded by 4% as market sentiment improves ahead of the White House Crypto Summit.

The combination of a declining U.S. dollar, historical patterns, and improving sentiment suggests Bitcoin may be gearing up for another move higher. Traders are now watching key resistance levels, with some analysts predicting Bitcoin could range between $85,000 and $95,000 before making another run at new highs.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $88,518.45.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Tariff Update: Canada Maintains Reciprocal Duties on U.S., Considers Additional Measures
data.description:
Canada is maintaining its reciprocal tariffs on American imports even after U.S. President Donald Trump has delayed for one month most of the 25% import duties applied to Canadian goods.

Trump has granted a tariff exemption on goods and services from Canada and Mexico that are covered by the existing United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) on trade that was struck during the president’s first term in office.

But despite Trumpâs rollbacks, Canada is keeping its current reciprocal tariffs on $30 billion worth of U.S. goods in place. The Canadian government has also said that it stands ready to move forward with a second round of tariffs that target $125 billion of U.S. imports into the country. Trump has said that the pause on tariffs imposed on Canadian and Mexican goods will only last until April 2.

Additional Measures

Canada is also considering additional trade actions against the U.S. Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, is moving forward with plans to apply a 25% surcharge to electricity exports sent to neighboring American states such as Michigan and Wisconsin starting on March 10.

British Columbia on Canada’s west coast is planning to implement a new toll on U.S. transport trucks traveling through the province en route to Alaska. Other Canadian government officials are publicly musing about restricting potash shipments to the U.S., which is a key ingredient in fertilizer used by farmers to grow crops such as corn and soybeans.

Agriculture analysts view potash as a crucial part of the food supply. There are only a few places that produce potash and Canada is the largest global supplier. The American Farm Bureau Federation says that more than 80% of potash used in the U.S. comes from Canada. Some economists are warning about the dangers of attacking Americaâs food supply and how the Trump administration might react.

Is the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF a Buy?

The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), which tracks the movements of the benchmark U.S. stock index has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 505 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 413 Buy, 86 Hold, and six Sell recommendations made in the last three months. The average VOO price target of $639.70 implies 21.07% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Is the SEC’s Decision to Drop the Coinbase Lawsuit a Win for Crypto?
data.description:
The SEC just dropped its lawsuit against Coinbase (COIN), but not everyone is convinced it’s a win for crypto. Some are questioning whether this is a true regulatory shift or a political move tied to the industry’s hefty donations. According to Cointelegraph, Trumpâs administration has received significant financial support from crypto players like Coinbase and Uniswap (UNI-USD). So, is Washington finally warming up to crypto, or is this just payback season?

Regulatory Uncertainty Still Looms

Even with the lawsuit dismissed, crypto companies arenât in the clear just yet. The SECâs stance on token securities remains murky, and industry insiders are waiting to see if the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) takes over regulatory oversight. A shift to the CFTC could signal a more pro-crypto stance from Washington, but thereâs no guarantee.

Stablecoin Regulation Could Be a Bigger Issue

A bigger issue might be stablecoin regulation and cryptoâs access to banking. Many businesses struggle to open accounts due to perceived money laundering risks. If FinCEN updates its guidelines, banks may finally start treating crypto companies like legitimate businesses. Until then, the regulatory uncertainty continues.

Coinbaseâs Rating Update Shows Market Uncertainty

As regulations change, analysts are reassessing Coinbaseâs outlook. 5-star Barclays analyst Benjamin Budish maintained a Hold rating on COIN stock and set a price target of $328, according to TipRanks. The stock closed at $235.38 last Friday, meaning Budishâs target implies significant upside.

Other analysts are split on Coinbaseâs trajectory. The stock holds a Moderate Buy consensus with a $341.83 average price target, suggesting a nearly 60% upside. Piper Sandler also maintained a Hold rating with a $310 target. Even though Coinbase is a key player in the crypto sector, the uncertainty surrounding U.S. regulation keeps analysts cautious.

Is Coinbase a Good Stock to Buy?

Analysts are cautiously bullish about COIN stock, with a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 11 Buys and nine Holds. Over the past year, COIN has decreased byÂ more than 10%, and theÂ average COIN price targetÂ of $341.83 implies an upside potential of 59.6% from current levels.

See more COIN analyst ratings
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: El Salvador’s Bitcoin Buying Continues Despite IMF Loan Agreement
data.description:
El Salvador isnât backing down from Bitcoin just yet. Despite agreeing to an International Monetary Fund (IMF) loan deal that restricts government Bitcoin purchases, President Nayib Bukele says the country will keep adding to its reserves. On March 4, Bukele confirmed El Salvador had bought another Bitcoin, raising eyebrows across the industry.

IMF Agreement Puts Bitcoin Buying in Question

El Salvador struck a $1.4 billion loan agreement with the IMF in January under the condition that it would stop accumulating Bitcoin and remove its status as compulsory legal tender. However, Bukeleâs recent purchase appears to contradict that. The IMF quickly clarified that the buy didnât violate the agreement, stating it had consulted with Salvadoran officials and received assurances that purchases remained within agreed terms, according to Reuters.

Why Did El Salvador Take the Deal?

Despite Bukeleâs strong pro-Bitcoin stance, analysts suggest that economic pressure forced El Salvadorâs hand. The countryâs debt has risen significantly in recent years, and the IMF loan was a necessary step to stabilize finances. With the April 30 deadline for the agreement approaching, Bukeleâs moves suggest heâs making last-minute acquisitions before restrictions fully kick in. Whether this strategy pays off remains to be seen.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $89,361.17.
data.author: Annika Masrani
